title: Usage History discovery/prototype
summary: Proved local Codex session history as the safe prototype source for recent active-account daily token buckets, while deferring production UI, persistence, and account/workspace scoping claims.
phaseAboutShort: Prove the first safe data-source path for future opt-in token usage history.
oneSentenceProductChange: CodexPill now has an evidence-backed prototype path for reading recent token usage buckets from local Codex session history.
closeoutDecision: Phase complete; refine the future opt-in Usage History feature before building UI.

productDataPathImage.alt: Product data path diagram for the Usage History discovery prototype

codeObjectMapImage.alt: Code object map diagram for the Codex session token usage scanner

changes.title: Local session history became the prototype source.
changes.explanation: The spike redirected away from backend usage endpoints and proved local Codex session JSONL token_count rows as the useful historical token source for this phase.
changes.proof: Findings and feature contract document source choice, useful fields, limits, and non-goals.

changes.title: A scanner boundary keeps Codex session parsing out of features.
changes.explanation: The prototype reader lives under Platform/Codex and converts session rows into day buckets without adding UI or persistent Usage History storage.
changes.proof: The scanner is isolated from SwiftUI/MenuBar code and follows the existing Codex adapter boundary.

changes.title: Synthetic parser coverage protects the privacy boundary.
changes.explanation: Tests use synthetic JSONL shapes rather than committed live session samples, covering totals, malformed rows, and day-window behavior.
changes.proof: Landing verification ran the full test suite successfully.

evidence.claim: The reviewed implementation landed exactly at the merge-ready head.
evidence.evidence: Review result and PR metadata both name 075774e79f089bfe2134a443b5b106e463a187e0; main was fast-forwarded to that SHA.
evidence.note: PR #126 is merged with merge commit equal to the reviewed head.

evidence.evidence: GitHub CI Test passed before landing; local landing gate make test passed with 534 tests in 63 suites.
evidence.note: No UI/live verification was required for this prototype.

evidence.claim: The phase stayed inside privacy and prototype boundaries.
evidence.evidence: Synthetic fixtures were used for tests, findings avoid raw session samples, and no production graph, menu UI, background collection, or persistent storage was added.
evidence.note: Raw auth and private response material remain out of committed artifacts.

evidence.claim: Future product direction still needs a scoping decision.
evidence.evidence: The report documents account/workspace scoping as ambiguous and saved-account querying as unsupported without future design.
evidence.note: This is intentional phase learning, not a shipping blocker.

safeToClaim.title: Prototype source
safeToClaim.body: Local Codex session JSONL token-count rows can support recent daily token bucket prototypes when those rows exist.

safeToClaim.title: Boundary
safeToClaim.body: Codex session parsing is isolated under Platform/Codex, with features consuming only shaped token bucket output later.

safeToClaim.title: Safety
safeToClaim.body: The landed artifacts use synthetic fixtures and aggregate findings, not committed raw auth, raw sessions, prompts, responses, account IDs, hostnames, or private paths.

doNotOverclaim.title: Account scope
doNotOverclaim.body: The phase does not prove whether history is Codex-specific, account-wide, workspace-scoped, or org-scoped in every environment.

doNotOverclaim.title: Saved accounts
doNotOverclaim.body: The phase does not support querying non-active saved accounts without a future isolated-auth or switching design.

doNotOverclaim.title: Product UI
doNotOverclaim.body: No production graph, menu cell, preferences entry, background collection, cost dashboard, or persistent Usage History store shipped.

followUp.title: Refine opt-in Usage History before UI work.
followUp.body: Define storage policy, active-account copy, empty/error states, local history retention caveats, and whether the account/workspace scoping ambiguity is acceptable for a future user-facing feature.

appendix.source: GitHub CI and local landing gate
